items.title: Reducing Temporal Redundancy for Efficient Vision-Language-Action Inference
items.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models exhibit strong generalization for robotic manipulation, yet their high inference latency limits real time deployment. We identify two primary sources of temporal redundancy in existing VLA pipelines: repeated visual encoding of highly similar consecutive frames and multi step iterative sampling in diffusion based policies. To address this, we propose a system level acceleration strategy that reduces computation in both perception and action generation. On the perception side, we incrementally update only tokens corresponding to dynamic scene regions instead of re-encoding entire frames. On the policy side, we compress diffusion sampling into a compact 2-step schedule through efficiency oriented training while preserving action precision. Experiments on Libero, RobotWin, and Real Robot Platforms demonstrate over 2 times speedup while maintaining high performance, achieving up to 98% success rate on general manipulation benchmarks. Our codes will be released on Github.
items.llm_evidence_en: reducing temporal redundancy for efficient VLA inference
items.llm_evidence_cn: 减少时间冗余以实现高效VLA推理
items.llm_evidence: 减少时间冗余以实现高效VLA推理
items.canonical_evidence: 减少时间冗余以实现高效VLA推理
items.llm_tldr_en: This work accelerates VLA inference by incrementally updating visual tokens and compressing diffusion sampling to 2 steps, achieving significant speedup.
items.llm_tldr_cn: VLA模型因高推理延迟难以实时部署。本文提出系统级加速策略：在感知端仅更新动态区域的token，在动作生成端将扩散采样压缩至2步。实验显示推理速度提升数倍，同时保持任务成功率。该工作为VLA模型的实际应用扫清了效率障碍。
items.llm_tldr: VLA模型因高推理延迟难以实时部署。本文提出系统级加速策略：在感知端仅更新动态区域的token，在动作生成端将扩散采样压缩至2步。实验显示推理速度提升数倍，同时保持任务成功率。该工作为VLA模型的实际应用扫清了效率障碍。
